source_lists.title: Post-Horror
source_lists.desc:
Post-horror - A modern (2014-) subgenre of horror cinema, that focuses on social issues, existential and mental health problems, setting up the mood and instilling terror into everyday situations using slow-burning build-up. It focuses on roots of horror movies, telling stories about unidentified evil of unknown origin or traditional folklore creatures, such as vampires, witches and ghosts. It throws away modern horror themes and conventions, such as jump-scares, stories about teenagers getting slaughtered, possesions and exorcisms and other cliches. The term was coined in 2017 by Steve Rose in his controversial article for The Guardian.

Suggestions very, very welcome!

source_lists.title: Post Horror
source_lists.desc:
Films mentioned as provisional corpus of post-horror cinema in Post Horror: Art Genre and Cultural Elevation by David Church (2021)


1 – 18: Primary/core texts
19 – 42: Secondary/peripheral texts

source_lists.title: post-horror
source_lists.desc:
post-horror: a cycle of horror films since ca 2014 that share similar aesthetic tendencies and (minimalist) stylistic traits that generate specific forms of negative affect, eg: dread.
this list is inspired by a david church's corpus with a few additions of my own.
